data.title: Communication services weekly roundup: Alphabet leads, The Trade Desk lags
data.description:
U.S. stocks wavered amid increased volatility as the U.S.-China trade war increased uncertainity, while the U.S. government shutdown entered its 17th day.
 
 
Overall, the sector, represented by the Communication Services Select Sector SPDR ETF (NYSEARCA:XLC), gained 1% in the week, while the broader market (S&P 500) added 1.7%.
 
 

Weekly top gainers

  • Alphabet (GOOGL) surged 7.07% and (GOOG) 6.86% in the week.
  • Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) moved up by 6.37%. WBD rose on report that a bid from Paramount Skydance (PSKY) may come as soon as this week.
  • AppLovin (APP) gained 5.16%.
  • Take-Two Interactive Software (TTWO) advanced 3.72%.

Biggest weekly decliners

  • The Trade Desk (TTD) fell 4.55% in the week.
  • Charter Communications (CHTR) slipped 2.44%.
  • Netflix (NFLX) dropped 1.70%.
  • Paramount Skydance (PSKY) shares lost 1.35%.
 

ETFs to tab the communication services sector include: (NYSEARCA:XLC), (VOX), (IYZ), (RSPC), and (XTL).

data.title: Weekly consumer staples check: Bungee Global and Estee Lauder tops chart
data.description:
Wall Street stocks wavered amid increased volatility on Friday but managed to resurge, as Wall Street was rattled by concerns on the health of regional banks and credit risks while the U.S. government shutdown continued.

Let us look at last week's best and worst performers in the consumer staples segment.

Top weekly gainers include:

  • Bungee Global (BG) +20.71% despite the company revising its 2025 adjusted EPS outlook to $7.30–$7.60, down from the prior estimate of ~$7.75, to reflect the Viterra integration and macro conditions. The company revised its segment and volume reporting structure to align with its value chain operating model following the merger with Viterra.
  • Estee Lauder (EL) +14.98% after Goldman Sachs upgrades to Buy from Hold, citing a turning corner and possible return to topline growth as soon as Q1 with double-digit EBIT margins by FY27.
  • Dollar Tree (DLTR) +10.08% reaffirmed its near-term guidance ahead of the retailer's Investor Day event. The company also noted that comparable same-store sales growth is up 3.8% so far in FQ3.
  • Keurig Dr Pepper (KDP) +8.56% after report that prominent  activist Starboard Value has taken a stake after its announced €15.7 billion deal for JDE Peet's was received poorly by investors. of 
  • Brown-Forman (BF.B) +7.53% CEO Lawson Whiting discussed the company’s ambition to double its size while also acknowledging that softer global spirits demand and external economic challenges will push back their timeline by one or two years beyond the original target date of 2032 target.

Top weekly losers include:  

  • Kenvue (KVUE) -8.17% after reports of claimants seeking damages from J&J (NYSE:JNJ) and Kenvue UK Limited, a unit of Kenvue (NYSE:KVUE), the consumer care spinoff of the New Brunswick, New Jersey-based pharma giant.
  • Hershey Company (HSY) -2.28%
  • Altria Group (MO) -2.24%
  • General Mills (GIS) -1.93%
  • Philip Morris (PM) -1.50%

ETFs linking to consumer staples: (NYSEARCA:VDC), (NYSEARCA:FXG), (NYSEARCA:IYK), (NYSEARCA:KXI), (NYSEARCA:FSTA), (NASDAQ:PSL).

data.title: Novo Nordisk GLP-1 pill now U.S. approved for heart disease risk
data.description:
Novo Nordisk (NVO) has received approval to market its oral GLP-1 medicine Rybelsus in the U.S. to reduce the risk of major cardiovascular events such as stroke in adults with type 2 diabetes, the Danish drugmaker announced.

Having first approved the pill in 2019 for use with diet and exercise to treat type 2 diabetes in adults, the FDA expanded its label to include patients at a higher risk for major adverse cardiovascular events, the company announced late Friday.

The approval marks the first time an oral GLP-1 medicine will be indicated in the U.S. to cut the risk of MACE events in this patient group, Novo (NVO) added.

The decision is based on data from the company’s Phase 3b SOUL trial, in which the once-daily therapy at a 14 mg dose led to a statistically significant 14% reduction in risk of MACE events compared to placebo at four years.

In 2024, the FDA approved Novo’s (OTCPK:NONOF) obesity therapy Wegovy to cut the risk of MACE events in obese or overweight adults with established heart disease. An oral form of the popular injectable is currently undergoing FDA review for the treatment of obesity, with a decision expected by the end of this year.

data.title: AGNC stock stands to benefit from tightening in spreads despite lower estimated Q3 earnings
data.description:
A bellwether for mortgage REITs, AGNC Investment (NASDAQ:AGNC), is scheduled to announce Q3 financial results on Monday, October 20th, before market open, and the stock stands to benefit from the recent tightening in spreads despite lower expected earnings.

The consensus EPS estimate is $0.39 (-9.3% Y/Y).

Over the last 2 years, AGNC has beaten EPS estimates 50% of the time. Over the last 3 months, EPS estimates have seen 1 upward revision and 7 downward.

Last quarter, earnings fell short of the average analyst estimate as tariff news roiled markets in the spring. While most markets bounced back, agency mortgage-backed securities did not.

"Although most asset class valuations retraced the April losses and ultimately increased quarter-over-quarter, agency MBS were an exception, as spreads to benchmark rates widened moderately during the second quarter. As a result of this underperformance, AGNC's economic return for the second quarter was -1.0%," said CEO Peter Federico.

"Looking ahead, we expect banks and foreign demand for agency MBS to grow. In addition, as we enter the third quarter, the seasonal supply pattern for MBS issuance should improve. We expect the net supply of new MBS will be about $200 billion this year, the low end of most forecasts. Since quarter end, MBS spreads have tightened slightly and are showing signs of stabilization," Federico had said during the second-quarter earnings call.

"We have started to see a positive shift in our mortgage universe with 30-year mortgage rates decreasing to 6.3% from 7%+ earlier in the year and agency spreads tightening materially," said Piper Sandler analyst Crispin Love in the Q3 mortgage finance preview.

"We expect key topics on the (NASDAQ:AGNC) earnings call to be on spreads, mortgage rates, investment opportunities, and GSE reform. Going forward, we believe AGNC can maintain its current dividend level, with AGNC generating mid-to-high teens returns," noted Love in the research note.

"We are maintaining our 3Q25 core EPS estimate of $0.38 and our 2025 and 2026 estimates of $1.57 and $1.54, respectively. However, we are increasing our 3Q25 TBV (tangible book value) estimate to $8.60 from $7.94 as agency spreads have tightened ~20 bps from mid-July to quarter end. We are also increasing our GAAP EPS estimate to $1.12 from $0.45," said Love.

Piper Sandler raised its price target on the stock to $10.50 from $10.00, citing the action to be driven by higher tangible book value given recent spread tightening.

Ahead of the Q3 earnings release, the average Wall Street analysts' rating on the stock stands at Buy.

On the other side, Seeking Alpha authors see the stock as Hold.

"AGNC aggressively bought up swaps when interest rates were super low. Those swaps have been maturing, and we can see AGNC's cost of funds drift upward," noted SA contributor Rida Morwa.

"It is important to note that through the end of 2025, the swaps expiring are going to have a larger financial impact than repo rates coming down," said Morwa.

"For traders who buy the rumor and sell the news, AGNC is going to move quickly into the category of no longer meeting any of their interests. This year, AGNC has strongly outperformed the market on the expectation of interest rate cuts, not that they're here, we're seeing traders exit their positions, bringing current returns closer to that of the overall market," noted Morwa.

"For income investors who position their portfolio to benefit regardless of what interest rates are doing, AGNC continues to be a highly attractive opportunity to collect double-digit yields paid out monthly from ultra-safe investments in agency MBS," according to the author.

The stock has added ~8.5% year-to-date, and closed 0.71% higher at $9.99 on Friday ahead of its Q3 earnings release.

data.title: Bond king Bill Gross warns about regional bank “cockroaches” and gold’s momentum rally
data.description:
Bond-market legend Bill Gross has warned about the recent rally in gold prices and argued that credit risk concerns that have caused a selloff among regional banks will continue to impact stocks and bonds.

His comments came after gold (XAUUSD:CUR) and silver (XAGUSD:CUR) prices reached new all-time highs early this week before sliding on Friday as U.S.-China trade tensions eased, with President Donald Trump noting that a "full-scale" tariff on Chinese imports would be unsustainable.

“Gold has become a momentum/meme asset. If you want to own it, wait a while,” the Pimco founder said in an X post on Friday as Gold Trust ETF (NYSEARCA:GLD) recorded its first intraday drop after five straight sessions of gains and a YTD rally of ~61%.

Meanwhile, the NYSE Arca Gold Miners Index (GDX) headed to its biggest intraday drop since May with a ~7% decline, with Newmont (NEM), Barrick (B), and Agnico Eagle Mines (AEM) among notable decliners.

“Regional bank 'cockroaches' may continue to affect stocks AND bonds,” Gross said regarding the financial sector, referring to a term JPMorgan Chase (JPM) CEO Jamie Dimon used earlier this week to identify the credit risk caused by the bankruptcy of car parts supplier First Brands Group.

“I probably shouldn’t say this, but when you see one cockroach, there are probably more,” Dimon said during the company’s Q3 earnings call on Tuesday, adding, “And so we should—everyone should be forewarned on this one.”

SPDR S&P Regional Banking ETF (NYSEARCA:KRE) fell ~6% on Thursday, driven by notable decliners such as Jefferies Financial (JEF), Zions Bancorporation (ZION), and Western Alliance (WAL), which also flagged credit losses.

However, the sector recovered some losses on Friday with largely positive commentary from banks, and as analysts pointed out, the industry appears to be on a solid footing, and credit risk events could not indicate a systemic issue.

data.title: Lufthansa follows Ryanair to cut German flight schedule amid high fees
data.description:
Lufthansa (OTCQX:DLAKF) has become the latest European carrier after Ryanair (NASDAQ:RYAAY) to plan a significant reduction in its flight schedule in Germany amid high aviation taxes and fees, Welt am Sonntag, a German publication, reported on Saturday.

Lufthansa (OTCQX:DLAKY) CEO Carsten Spohr stated during an interview that his company would eliminate about 100 domestic German flights per week this summer. “Without a reduction in location costs, further cuts will be unavoidable,” he added.

"Connections like those from Munich to Münster/Osnabrück or from Munich to Dresden are under review," he said, noting that those routes operate at a loss to the company. He added that flight operations within the country are not economically viable due to a two-fold rise in state costs since 2019.

On Wednesday, Irish budget carrier Ryanair (OTCPK:RYAOF) announced plans to reduce German winter capacity by 800,000 seats and cancel 24 routes to nine airports, including Hamburg, citing high aviation tax and access costs.

data.title: J.B. Hunt tops weekly industrial gainers while Fastenal bleeds after Q3 results
data.description:
The Industrial Select Sector SPDR Fund ETF (XLI) was flat this week, at +0.18%. On a year-to-date basis, XLI has been slightly ahead of the broader markets, gaining 15.16% compared to the S&P500’s 13.30% return.

Weekly top 5 gainers

  • J.B. Hunt Transport Services (NASDAQ:JBHT) soared 20.25% this week, gaining the most among industrial stocks after  topping Q3 estimates. The company reported an EPS of $1.76 and a revenue of $3.05B.
  • Generac (NYSE:GNRC) widened its gains in the week, ending +12%.
  • Caterpillar (NYSE:CAT) added 7.28% after  Oppenheimer named it its top sector pick, rating it Outperform. Caterpillar also announced it plans to  acquire RPMGlobal Holdings.
  • Southwest Airlines (NYSE:LUV) was +7.13% in the week. The company said that it has  partnered with Volantio, a post-booking revenue management firm, to improve efficiency, optimize demand, and provide passengers with more travel options.
  • Expeditors International of Washington (NYSE:EXPD) was +6%.

Weekly top 5 losers

  • Fastenal (NASDAQ:FAST) fell the most this week among industrial stocks, -7.25%, after  missing Q3 profit estimates.
  • Northrop Grumman (NYSE:NOC) lost 4.65% among other defense stocks after the U.S. Treasury Department  said that the government may ask defense companies to scale back share repurchases amid rising federal spending on national security.
  • Axon Enterprise (NASDAQ:AXON) was down 4.60%.
  • Verisk Analytics (NASDAQ:VRSK) continued to lose this week, ending -4.41%.
  • L3Harris Technologies (NYSE:LHX) -2.90%.

ETFs to tab the industrial sector include: (XLI), (VIS), (XAR), (EXI), (PSCI), (PRN), (FIDU).

data.title: Insider trades: CVS Health, Fastenal among major names this week
data.description:
A weekly overview of insider trading, highlighting significant purchases and sales by investors, directors, and executives. The transactions occurred between October 13, 2025 and October 17, 2025.

  • Albemarle’s (NYSE:ALB) General Counsel & Secretary Ander Krupa  sold 116 shares at $95 per share for a total value of $11,020. Krupa continues to own 11,655 shares in the firm.
  • Credo Technology Group (NASDAQ:CRDO) Director Pantas Sutardja  sold 4,408 shares at $147.41 each for $649,761. Following the sale, his ownership has reduced to 5.89M shares.
  • D-Wave Quantum’s (NYSE:QBTS) President and CEO Alan Baratz  disposed of 31,369 shares at $43.74 apiece, worth $1.37M. He holds 2.80M shares in the firm post-sale.
  • William Milmoe, 10% owner at Celsius Holdings (NASDAQ:CELH), let go of 40,000 shares at $62.50 for $2.5M, cutting his holdings to 221,245 shares.
  • On the buy side, CVS Health’s (NYSE:CVS) EVP and Chief People Officer Heidi Capozzi  bought 80 shares of the company in prices ranging between $56.47 and $62.24, spending a total of $4,588.
  • Fastenal’s (NASDAQ:FAST) Director Sam Hseng-Hung Hsu  purchased company shares worth $42,450. He bought 1,000 shares for about $42.45 each, raising his holdings to 9,000 shares.

data.title: BITF, ABTC, other crypto-tied stocks lead week's financials movers
data.description:
Crypto-focused firms were among the biggest winners and losers across the broader financials space in the week ended Oct. 17.

Bitfarms (BITF), rising the most of any financial stock (with market cap over $2B) this week, surged 19% as the crypto miner named a new CFO to lead financial strategy for its North American AI expansion;

Hut 8 (HUT), another bitcoin miner focused on AI infrastructure, jumped 11%; 

Alternative asset manager DigitalBridge Group (DBRG) climbed 10.6%;

Cipher Mining (CIFR) also gained 10.6%; and

American Express (AXP) rounded out the five biggest weekly gainers with a 9.6% advance, after the credit card lender and network raised the lower end of its 2025 guidance after better-than-expected Q3 earnings.

Conversely, American Bitcoin (ABTC), a bitcoin mining company backed by U.S. President Donald Trump's oldest sons, Eric Trump and Donald Trump Jr., was the biggest financial loser, tumbling 20%;

Baldwin Insurance Group (BWIN) dropped 19.5%;

Crypto exchange Gemini Space Station (GEMI) gapped down 16%;

Chinese financial services firm Lufax Holdings (LU) retreated 12%; and 

Blockchain-based lender Figure Technology Solutions (FIGR) slid 9%.

data.title: Gucci owner Kering is said to be nearing deal to sell Beauty unit to L’Oréal
data.description:
• Kering (OTCPK:PPRUF), the French luxury product maker behind famous brands like Gucci, is in discussions to sell its beauty business to its local rival L’Oréal (OTCPK:LRLCF) (OTCPK:LRLCY), The Wall Street Journal reported on Saturday, citing people familiar with the matter.
  • A potential transaction is expected to be announced as early as next week, assuming the talks won't fall apart and there won’t be another bidder, the people said, adding that the deal could fetch a valuation of roughly $8B for the unit.
  • The report comes only weeks after Kering’s (OTCPK:PPRUY) new chief executive, Luca de Meo, a former CEO of French carmaker Renault (OTCPK:RNLSY) (OTCPK:RNSDF), assumed the company’s leadership in September.
